device_name: uCT ATLAS with uWS-CT-Dual Energy Analysis
company: Shanghai United Imaging Healthcare Co., Ltd.
intended_use: uCT ATLAS is a computed tomography x-ray system, which is intended to produce cross-sectional images of the whole body by computer reconstruction of x-ray transmission data taken at different angles a
intended_use_full: uCT ATLAS is a computed tomography x-ray system, which is intended to produce cross-sectional images of the whole body by computer reconstruction of x-ray transmission data taken at different angles and planes. uCT ATLAS is applicable to head, whole body, cardiac, and vascular x-ray Computed Tomography. uCT ATLAS has the capability to image a whole organ in a single rotation. Organs include, but not limited to head, heart, liver, kidney, pancreas, joints, etc. uCT ATLAS is intended to be used for low dose CT lung cancer screening for the early detection of lung nodules that may represent cancer. uWS-CT-Dual Energy Analysis software uses UIH CT images acquired using different tube voltages and/or tube currents of the same anatomical location to enable images to be generated at multiple energies within the available spectrum.
target_condition: Lung cancer screening, cardiac imaging, vascular imaging, kidney stone characterization, gout assessment, and general body imaging across head, thorax, abdomen, and pelvis
ground_truth: Comparison to predicate device (uCT 760, uCT 780) and reference devices (Aquilion ONE Vision, Deep Recon, DUAL ENERGY SYSTEM PACKAGE, DISCOVERY CT750 HD)
other_metrics: Non-clinical testing included dosimetry and image performance tests; performance verification included evaluation reports for functions and clinical image evaluation of applications; quantitative dose reduction claims made but specific values not reported in this summary
